title: Andrea Pauw
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Andrea Pauw
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_department: Modern and Classical Languages and Literatures

organizational_affiliations.label: Modern and Classical Languages and Literatures

biography: Dr. Andrea Pauw received her Ph.D. in Spanish from the University of Virginia in 2020. A specialist in early modern Iberian literatures and cultures, her research examines aljamiado (Spanish written with the Arabic alphabet) manuscripts produced by moriscos (Muslims forcibly converted to Christianity in the sixteenth century) through the lens of linguistic anthropology. Her research has been published in the Hispanic Review and in Routledge’s Companion to Medieval Iberia: Unity in Diversity . Dr. Pauw’s current book project examines representations of moriscos in nineteenth-century history writing, visual art, and the press, arguing that the confluence of interest in the moriscos’ past and discoveries of aljamiado manuscripts profoundly shaped the emergence of the modern Spanish nation-state.
education_entries: Ph D in Spanish, University of Virginia
MA in Spanish, University of Virginia
BA in Hispanic Studies, Davidson College
research_interests: Premodern Iberian Literatures and Cultures Moriscos and Aljamiado Manuscript Production Linguistic Anthropology 19 th -century Cultural and Intellectual History
teaching_interests: Intermediate Spanish Language Introduction to Spanish Literature I and II Advanced Spanish Conversation Techniques of Translation
